title: Geometry of Delta-Matroids
speaker: Matt W Larson
abstract: Delta-matroids are "type B" or "type C" analogues of matroids. I will discuss how to extend a geometric construction related to matroids to delta-matroids. Using this construction, we prove the ultra log-concavity of the number of independent sets of a large class of delta-matroids. This gives inequalities which are new even for matroids. Joint work with Chris Eur, Alex Fink, and Hunter Spink.

proof_stages.label: Lagrangian realization of delta-matroids
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up the symplectic vector space with barred indices, defines Lagrangian subspaces and admissible Pluecker coordinates, and recalls Galashin's polytope criterion that turns D(L) into a delta-matroid.

proof_stages.label: Independent sets and shelterability
proof_stages.summary: Independent sets and their numbers I_k are defined, and the shelterability condition is introduced as the existence of an ordinary matroid on the doubled ground set certifying delta-matroid independence.

proof_stages.label: Log-concavity theorem and conjectural target
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem is stated as two incomparable strengthened log-concavity inequalities for shelterable delta-matroids, with the talk focusing on the first inequality and explaining the conjectural nonshelterable extension.

proof_stages.label: Type-B toric vector-bundle construction
proof_stages.summary: On the type-B permutohedral toric variety X_Bn, a rank-2n bundle M is built from two toric O(1)-models on each P1 factor, and each Lagrangian L gives an exact sequence 0 → S_L → M → Q_L → 0 whose total Chern class depends only on D(L), allowing c(Q_D) to be defined combinatorially.

proof_stages.label: Top Chern class as the number of bases
proof_stages.summary: The top Chern degree is interpreted as a generic zero-count and then computed by equivariant localization on X_Bn, where the piecewise-polynomial value is nonzero precisely on orthants corresponding to delta-matroid bases, giving c_n(Q_D) = I_n.

proof_stages.label: Lower Chern intersections count independent sets
proof_stages.summary: Divisor classes y_i pulled back from the P1 factors restrict to smaller type-B toric varieties, so iterating the top-Chern computation gives intersection formulas such as ∫(y_1+...+y_n)^k c_{n-k}(Q_D) = k! I_{n-k}.

proof_stages.label: Realizable case via mixed intersections
proof_stages.summary: For realizable D = D(L), the quotient Q_L is presented by a globally generated bundle, the projectivized kernel P(K_L) converts the Chern-class integrals into mixed intersections of positive divisors, and Khovanskii-Teissier log-concavity yields the desired inequality.

proof_stages.label: Shelterable extension through Bergman positivity
proof_stages.summary: For arbitrary delta-matroids a combinatorial cycle class [P(K_D)] is defined with the same intersection formula; when D is shelterable it is related to the Bergman class of the sheltering matroid, importing known Hodge-positivity results and completing the proof for shelterable D while leaving the fully general case open.

examples.name: Matroid lift as v direct-sum v-perp
examples.note: The construction v ⊕ v^⊥ illustrates how ordinary matroids sit inside delta-matroids and later makes the theorem concrete as a statement about independent and spanning pairs in a matroid.
examples.engagement_reason: he uses the lift/direct-sum construction concretely, identifying the sheltering matroid as a direct sum with the dual and deriving what the independent-set numbers count in the lifted case
